demographic_audit_note: Audited face sheet against Atlantis; found and corrected a digit transposition in the primary phone number and verified Medicare Advantage PPO status. Confirmed home address in 19143 zip code is within MA travel radius.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirmed the discharge goal is Home with HHA support, though noted the patient's spouse is 'very nervous' about the recovery process.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: SW reports PT/OT are clearing patient for discharge; PN conducted bedside education with patient and spouse. Spouse expressed concern regarding medication management post-bypass.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into patient's smartphone and physical program flyer delivered to spouse during education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached spouse 24 hours prior to discharge; triggered 'Caregiver_Panic' signal as she initially threatened to refuse discharge due to fear of surgical site care. PN de-escalated by walking through the Day 1 HHA arrival schedule.
atlantis_final_sync: Confirmed D/C date of 11/18 and scheduled first visit date of 11/19 entered into Atlantis.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant (MA) scheduled for 11/19 at 11:00 AM; physical appointment card handed to patient during final bedside check-in.
narrative_summary: PN identified the 75yo male in the Atlantis queue following cardiac bypass and verified all demographics for HHA referral. During the maintenance phase, PN addressed significant caregiver anxiety through program education and Day 1 expectations. Despite a last-minute panic from the spouse, PN secured the transition by coordinating the MA first visit within the 24-hour window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Caregiver Panic: Spouse expresses fear of managing post-CABG wound and meds.
HHA Intake Coordinator Unavailable: Single point of contact at agency out sick.

format_2_triples.situation: Spouse is overwhelmed by the complexity of post-discharge cardiac instructions.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked spouse through 'Day 1 expectations' including HHA arrival and equipment check.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis queue displays an outdated emergency contact for the patient.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited demographics against the facility face sheet and updated Atlantis record.

format_2_triples.situation: Spouse threatens to refuse discharge due to fear of managing the patient's care.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the volatile family situation to the Social Worker for immediate intervention.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN steps back and allows the Social Worker to lead the conversation regarding the spouse's anxiety, deferring any discussion of home care until the SW provides a clinical update.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary by letting the professional in charge of discharge planning manage family dynamics before the PN introduces service logistics.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN provides the spouse with a concrete 'Day 1' schedule, including the exact time window for the HHA nurse and the MA visit, and hands over a physical appointment card.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows Stage 2 and 3 checklist items for education and handoff, reducing caregiver anxiety through clear, non-clinical logistics.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The PN reviews the patient's cardiac medication list with the spouse and suggests a simplified pill-organizer strategy to make the discharge more manageable.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the Banned Actions list by managing facility/clinical medications, which is outside the PN scope and risks patient safety.
